title: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK conditional compiles different TX behavior
description:
lichen_l2.c:221-224: When HAVE_LICHEN_LINK is 0, the code sends raw IPv6 over LoRa without any framing or MAC.

The comment says 'for testing' but this path is production-compiled when CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK is not set.

What goes wrong:
1. No MIC/CRC protection - packets can be corrupted without detection
2. No SCHC compression - 40-byte IPv6 header sent uncompressed, eating MTU
3. No replay protection
4. Interoperability: other LICHEN nodes expect framed packets

A user who builds without CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK (perhaps due to missing dependency) gets a silently broken network.

Fix: Consider making LICHEN_LINK mandatory (BUILD_ASSERT), or at minimum log WRN at startup when running in raw mode. The existing LOG_WRN at line 475 ('RAW MODE - no framing/crypto') is good but may be missed in log scrollback.
close_reason: Fixed: Added BUILD_ASSERT(HAVE_LICHEN_LINK) to fail build without CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK. Raw IPv6-over-LoRa mode is now blocked at compile time with clear error message explaining the security/interop implications.
